methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Path Robotics is a Series D robotics and manufacturing company founded by Andy and Alex Lonsberry that has raised $300M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comp (277810.KQ), including same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies such as Exotec ($2.0B Series D), Divergent ($2.3B Series E), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $300M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $450M. Peer-median analysis across the four primary comparables produces a median valuation of $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.3B. The peer set reflects meaningful stage and sector alignment, providing a substantive anchor to market-tested valuations.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $382M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide spread reflects inherent tension between stage-progression mechanics applied to disclosed capital and observed peer valuations at comparable development stages. Key uncertainties include Path Robotics' revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to named peers, customer concentration, and technology differentiation in a crowded sector. Resolution of these factors would narrow the range substantially.
